issues.evidence: Old Zheng appears as the Qinghe outer-warehouse material controller with two men and a compromised brother. He remains an outer-warehouse gatekeeper, not a full faction reveal.
issues.suggested_fix: In revision and acceptance, record Old Zheng as an outer-warehouse material-control figure. Do not expand him into Qinghe's full political order yet.

issues.evidence: The RV gains usable items: industrial sealant, clamps, curtain material, limited medical supplies, a small oil amount, and filter-edge material. The chapter explicitly says they are contaminated externally, limited, or insufficient to fully restore vehicle systems.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep resource updates qualitative or limited. Do not treat these materials as a full repair, full filter recovery, or medical-module unlock.

issues.evidence: Old Zheng asks to be carried to the Qinghe main building. This is a directional pressure beat but could imply broader Qinghe geography.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep it as a named destination only. Do not add interior order, floor layout, factions, or medical station details in chapter 7 revision.

proposed_state_updates: Old Zheng is introduced as the Qinghe outer-warehouse material controller who controls the warehouse door, key/access, and immediate supplies.
Old Zheng attempts to trade fuel, medicine, sealant, clamps, curtain material, and filter-edge material for boarding access for himself and two men.
Lu Chenzhou establishes the early vehicle-economy rule that resources can be traded, inspected, and priced, but cannot directly buy RV boarding qualification.

Lin Zhixia verifies the medicine and isolation materials, identifying incomplete or poor-quality supplies: expired fever medicine, no initial suture kit, low disinfectant, moldy curtain material, and contaminated outer packaging.

The RV gains limited usable supplies: industrial sealant, clamps, curtain/isolation material, basic medical supplies including suture thread and gloves, a small amount of fuel, and partial filter-edge material.
The gained materials are limited and/or externally contaminated; they do not fully repair the RV, fully restore filter efficiency, unlock the medical module, or create a free upgrade.
Old Zheng reveals that one of his men has gray-eye and confusion symptoms; Lin Zhixia judges him as suspected mutation and recommends isolation without allowing him into the RV.
Lu Chenzhou refuses to open the RV door or let Old Zheng's people enter the living space; observation, isolation, and boarding rules remain intact.
Old Zheng retaliates by broadcasting the RV's position and clean-filter value through the outer-warehouse speaker / communication system.
Fog-side vehicle lights, metal striking sounds, and system warnings show that external human and mist-pressure threats are gathering near the outer warehouse.
human_approval_needed: Approve Old Zheng as the Qinghe outer-warehouse material controller without treating him as a full Qinghe faction reveal.
Approve the early economy rule: material trade can buy goods or limited service, but cannot buy RV boarding qualification.
Approve the limited resource gain from chapter 7 as usable but insufficient for full repair, full filter recovery, or medical-module unlock.
Approve Old Zheng's broadcast of the RV location as the next pressure hook toward fog-wall backflow / car-seizure escalation.
